name: on-policy-self-distillation
description: Apply On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression to on-policy problems. Technique: Reasoning models think out loud, but much of what they say is noise. We introduc...
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression".

Key contribution: Reasoning models think out loud, but much of what they say is noise. We introduce OPSDC (On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression), a method that teaches models to reason more concisely by distilling their own concise behavior back into themselves.

## When to use

- Apply when solving on-policy problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05433
